title: P2 batch: consistency audit + path-drift warn + related graph polish
description: Ship P2-8 cross-artifact consistency audit hooks, P2-9 relevance_paths drift diagnostic warn-only UX, and P2-11 related graph readability polish in one feature batch. No Obsidian UI; no pure-docs P2-10 work as a code task.
action: Enhance (1) packages/cli/__tests__/ai-client-policy-drift.test.ts + packages/cli/src/commands/audit.ts / doctor-check-registry for cross-artifact consistency signals (bootstrap/skills/config fixtures), (2) packages/server/src/services/doctor-relevance-paths.ts messages remain status warn/info only with clearer actionHint including stable_id + glob samples, (3) packages/server/src/services/doctor-related-graph.ts + packages/cli/src/store/knowledge-doctor-checks.ts + i18n doctor.store.related-broken / related-hub for readable samples and remediation pointing to fab_review modify — never error/fixable.
scope: packages/server doctor + packages/cli audit/store diagnostics + i18n

criteria: doctor-relevance-paths.ts still emits knowledge_relevance_paths_drift / dangling / narrow_no_paths only as warn or info (rg status/kind never 'error' for these three codes)
doctor-relevance-paths.test.ts covers drift message includes stable_id or path sample and remains non-fixable
doctor.store.related-broken i18n (en+zh) includes remediation mentioning `fab_review` and sample edges `source → target` format remains

packages/cli/__tests__/ai-client-policy-drift.test.ts contains a new assertion block named `peer-micro-transfer consistency` (or describe title containing that string) covering bootstrap-canonical pre-action/archive wording vs fabric-recall-playbook SKILL.md presence of `fab_recall(paths=`
pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server test -- doctor-relevance-paths exits 0; pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test -- ai-client-policy-drift exits 0
convergence.verification: pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server test -- doctor-relevance-paths; pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test -- ai-client-policy-drift doctor-checks; rg -n 'related-broken|relevance_paths_drift|status: "warn"' packages/server/src/services/doctor-relevance-paths.ts packages/cli/src/store/knowledge-doctor-checks.ts packages/shared/src/i18n/locales
convergence.definition_of_done: Operators get clearer warn-only path-drift and related-graph signals plus a tighter cross-artifact consistency audit hook; no graph UI.

files.change: Polish human messages to lead with stable_id, show top-N globs, keep kind warning/info; ensure actionHint is pasteable (edit frontmatter relevance_paths / fab_review modify)
Assert drift/dangling issues are not error severity; message contains identifying stable_id/path
Optional: sort broken links; cap samples; expose counts already present — pure polish, no new graph product surface
Improve samples formatting and overflow text; keep codes related_graph_broken_link (warn) and related_graph_hub (info)
Readable copy: counts, samples, remediation via fab_review modify / edit relevance_paths
Add checks that bootstrap-canonical template references fab_recall and fabric-archive skill template exists; parity-matrix still lists both clients' skill paths — lightweight consistency hooks

files.target: optional audit subcommand help / retired consistency
files.change: Only if a natural audit surface exists for consistency; prefer strengthening existing drift test over new heavy subcommand
Only if new consistency check is registered; otherwise leave unchanged

implementation: P2-9: read existing dangling/drift/narrow_no_paths builders; improve message templates without changing severity to error
P2-11: polish related-broken samples (source → target) and hub top-N; update en/zh i18n
P2-8: extend ai-client-policy-drift tests as the cheap consistency audit hook; avoid building Spex/Trellis dashboards
Do not implement Obsidian vault UI or full knowledge graph product
Skip P2-10 pure validation doc-only work (out of scope for implementation tasks)

reference.pattern: doctor-relevance-paths warn builders; BORROW-007 related graph diagnostics; ai-client-policy-drift parity matrix tests
reference.examples: related_graph_broken_link severity warn; knowledge_relevance_paths_drift status warn

rationale.chosen_approach: Batch independent P2 polish items into one agent task to minimize spawn overhead; each is small UX/diagnostic work on existing seams.
rationale.tradeoffs: Single task mixes three concerns — mitigated by clear implementation bullets and separate convergence criteria per concern

risks: Accidentally promoting drift to error — forbid in convergence
Scope creep into graph UI — explicit non-goal
Heavy new audit subcommand — prefer test/registry hooks
